title: Riding & Mounting Entities
description: Learn how to allow players to ride and control entities, spawn mounts, and manage passengers in Minecraft plugins.

lessons.title: Basic Riding Mechanics
lessons.content:
## Introduction
Players can ride entities like horses, pigs, boats, or custom mobs. Bukkit provides methods to manage passengers.

### Example: Mount a Player on a Horse
```java
Horse horse = player.getWorld().spawn(player.getLocation(), Horse.class);
horse.addPassenger(player);
```

### Logic Explanation
- `spawn(Location, Class)` creates the entity.
- `addPassenger(Entity)` puts the player on the entity.
- Multiple passengers can be added for some entities (e.g., boats).

### Edge Cases & Warnings
- Some entities cannot be ridden (e.g., creepers).
- Removing the entity removes the passenger.
- Check for nulls and ensure entity is loaded before mounting.

quiz.question: What does `setInvisible(true)` do to the mount?
Why should scheduled particle tasks be used carefully?
Can a player ride multiple passengers on a single horse?
options: No, horses allow only one passenger
